title: Iliad, Book 2: The Catalogue of Ships
description: Book 2 of Homer's Iliad: The Catalogue of Ships.
introduction: Book 2 of the Iliad: The Catalogue of Ships. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.original_text: Ἄλλοι μέν ῥα θεοί τε καὶ ἀνέρες ἱπποκορυσταὶ εὗδον παννύχιοι, Δία δ᾿ οὐκ ἔχε νήδυμος ὕπνος.
translation.text: Now the other gods and armored warriors slept through the night, but sweet sleep did not hold Zeus. He pondered in his heart how he might honor Achilles and destroy many beside the ships of the Achaeans. He sent a dream to Agamemnon, a false dream that promised victory.
